title: Submodules, Nested Repositories, and Dependency Boundaries · Guided Repository Studio
summary: A pairing session predicts each repository-state transition before building the first submodule dependency and trust manifest for submodules, nested repositories, and dependency boundaries. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of submodule dependency and trust manifest while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Explain gitlink entries, superproject commits, submodule repositories, checked-out commits, .gitmodules, local config, and detached submodule HEAD.
Predict init, update, sync, set-url, set-branch, recursive, remote, merge, rebase, and shallow behavior across configuration layers.
Stage gitlink changes, publish dependency commits before superproject refs, review nested diffs, and verify that every referenced object is reachable.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model
steps.instruction:
workshop case — Case G34 is a workshop in a disposable repository. the release mismatch comes from an uninitialized nested gitlink rather than branch drift. The learner must predict prefixes and object IDs expose initialized, mismatched, and conflicted nested states, run git submodule status --recursive, explain the parser submodule is uninitialized and its recorded gitlink names c0ffee0, test this changed condition: make the referenced dependency commit unreachable remotely and fail the release transfer gate, and preserve this recovery boundary: archive superproject and reachable dependency refs before URL or gitlink migration.

What must be established before implementation?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for explain gitlink entries, superproject commits, submodule repositories, checked-out commits, .gitmodules, local config, and detached submodule head.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact
steps.instruction: Append an Advanced Git evidence block headed "# Evidence: gita-submodule-alternative-decision-1ijns7" with exact before and after refs; a falsifiable prediction; the bounded simulator command; expected, observed, tree, behavior, changed-case, and recovery evidence; and one honest real-repository transfer limit.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Gita Submodule Alternative Decision into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: gita-submodule-alternative-decision-1ijns7
# scenario-key: trace-1ijns7 probe-7snji1 boundary-jns71i owner-1ijns7x fault-1ijns7z
# before-ref: main=a11ce1i candidate=b00b1s7
# hypothesis: the release mismatch comes from an uninitialized nested gitlink rather than branch drift
git submodule status --recursive
# expected: prefixes and object IDs expose initialized, mismatched, and conflicted nested states
# observation: the parser submodule is uninitialized and its recorded gitlink names c0ffee0
# changed-case: make the referenced dependency commit unreachable remotely and fail the release transfer gate
# after-ref: main=a11ce1i candidate=c0ffee7
# tree-proof: the affected tree and path set match the predicted repository transition
# behavior-proof: a changed input preserves the named product or release invariant
# recovery: archive superproject and reachable dependency refs before URL or gitlink migration
# transfer-limit: never execute untrusted nested hooks, builds, or recursive commands outside isolation
